skill.name: firecrawl-map
skill.description: Discover website URLs with Firecrawl map, including optional search filters and output files.
skill.summary: Map website URLs and find specific pages with Firecrawl.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported 19 issues, but most are false positives from Markdown backticks, example text, relative documentation links, and the word Description. The skill does intentionally allow Firecrawl CLI commands that can access websites and write output files, so it carries medium operational risk. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, credential theft, or hidden data exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Network-Capable External CLI Use
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash execution for firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands, and its examples run map commands against user-supplied URLs. This is legitimate for a website mapping skill, but it can make outbound network requests and write local output files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools block and command examples directly confirm external CLI execution with network-facing URL arguments. I found no evidence of malicious targets, secret collection, or hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick detections occur in Markdown inline code, a fenced bash example, an options table, and usage tips. These are documentation examples, not executable Ruby backtick calls inside a program file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines are clearly Markdown documentation and not source code that will execute automatically. The skill still documents commands, but these scanner hits are not Ruby backtick execution.

low_findings.title: Example URL Is Not Evidence of Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL finding points to docs.example.com in an illustrative tip. It is an example domain used to explain workflow, not a suspicious receiver for user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in explanatory text next to sample map and scrape usage. No evidence found that data is sent to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings are relative Markdown links to neighboring Firecrawl skill documentation. They do not read files, write files, or pass untrusted paths to a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sequences appear only in Markdown links under See also. There is no runtime filesystem operation connected to these relative paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Detection Is a Text Match False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings point to prose and table text, including the word Description. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage appears in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain metadata and a Markdown table header, not cryptographic logic. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA1, or other weak algorithm usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Command Surface
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands. This command surface is expected, but users should review target URLs and output paths before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools entries explicitly permit these commands. Risk depends on user-supplied URLs and paths rather than hidden code in the skill.

content.value_statement: Large websites make it hard to find the right page before scraping or research. This skill uses Firecrawl map to discover URLs, filter by search terms, and save results for follow-up work.
actual_capabilities: Runs Firecrawl map commands for a specified website URL.
Filters discovered pages with the Firecrawl search option.
Limits the number of returned URLs for large websites.
Supports JSON output for structured URL lists.
Writes mapping results to a chosen output file.
Guides follow-up scraping after the correct URL is found.
limitations: Requires the Firecrawl CLI or npx access to run commands.
Results depend on the target site availability and crawl permissions.
Does not extract page content by itself.
May miss pages that are blocked, private, or generated only after interaction.

use_cases.title: Find Documentation Pages
use_cases.description: Locate authentication, API, pricing, or setup pages on a large documentation site.

use_cases.title: Prepare Research URL Lists
use_cases.description: Create a focused list of public pages before reviewing or scraping a website.

use_cases.title: Plan Crawl Scope
use_cases.description: Check site structure and choose limits before running a broader crawl job.

prompt_templates.title: List Site URLs
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the pages on https://example.com and save up to 200 URLs for review.

prompt_templates.title: Search for One Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl map on https://docs.example.com and search for authentication pages.

prompt_templates.title: Create Structured Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Map https://example.com with a limit of 500 URLs and save the results as JSON.

prompt_templates.title: Scope a Follow-Up Scrape
prompt_templates.prompt: Map https://docs.example.com, identify the most relevant API reference URLs, and recommend which pages to scrape next.

output_examples.input: Map a documentation site and search for authentication.
output_examples.output: A short list of matching authentication-related URLs, with the strongest candidate highlighted for scraping.

output_examples.input: List up to 500 pages from a public marketing site.
output: Homepage and product pages are included.
Blog and help center URLs are separated for review.
The URL list is saved to the requested output file.

output_examples.input: Check a site before starting a crawl.
output_examples.output: A scoped summary of discovered sections, suggested crawl limits, and pages that need targeted scraping.

best_practices: Confirm the target domain before running a map command.
Use search filters when you need one topic from a large site.
Save results under a project folder such as .firecrawl for easier review.
anti_patterns: Do not map sites when you do not have permission or a legitimate reason.
Do not use very high limits before checking the site size.
Do not treat URL discovery as proof that page content was extracted.

faq.question: Does this skill scrape page content?
faq.answer: No. It discovers URLs. Use a scrape or crawl skill after selecting the pages you need.

faq.question: Can it find one page on a large site?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the search option with a focused term such as authentication, pricing, or setup.

faq.question: Does it support structured output?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented command supports JSON output for reusable URL lists.

faq.question: Why does it need network access?
faq.answer: It must contact the target website through Firecrawl to discover available URLs.

faq.question: Can it include subdomains?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented options include a flag for subdomain URL discovery.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning. The risk comes from intended network-capable CLI use, not confirmed malicious behavior.
